scope.context_note: Volume II section from an undergraduate real analysis note set. The local mathematical setting is chapter 'Arithmetic Operations and Relations', section 'Algebraic Structures'. The audit goal is in-depth learning of the structure: direct dependencies should be concepts needed to state each node precisely, with common ambient structure carried by the nearest appropriate ancestor.

review_instructions.order: Nodes are topologically sorted so dependencies and ambient ancestors appear before their users when possible.
review_instructions.state: Use reorder/state/working-delta.json as cumulative accepted state across all batches.
review_instructions.checker: Run reorder/tools/check_dependency_edge.py before accepting any add/remove.

nodes.title: Ordered Field Addition Law
nodes.statement_display:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$,
\[
a<b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+c<b+c.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$,
\[
a<b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+c<b+c.
\]

nodes.title: Ordered Field Multiplication Law
nodes.statement_display:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$,
\[
c>0 \Longrightarrow
\bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow a<b\bigr),
\]
and
\[
c<0 \Longrightarrow
\bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow b<a\bigr).
\]
Moreover, positive elements are closed under multiplication:
\[
0<a \land 0<b \Longrightarrow 0<ab.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$,
\[
c>0 \Longrightarrow
\bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow a<b\bigr),
\]
and
\[
c<0 \Longrightarrow
\bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow b<a\bigr).
\]
Moreover, positive elements are closed under multiplication:
\[
0<a \land 0<b \Longrightarrow 0<ab.
\]

nodes.title: Ordered Field Trichotomy
nodes.statement_display:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$ and $b$, exactly one of the following
holds:
\[
a=b,\qquad a<b,\qquad b<a.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$ and $b$, exactly one of the following
holds:
\[
a=b,\qquad a<b,\qquad b<a.
\]

nodes.title: Semigroup
nodes.statement_display: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.
nodes.statement_tex: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

direct_dependencies.title: Associativity
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Binary Closure
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Associativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Closure
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Associativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Closure
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

ancestor_tree.title: Semigroup
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

dependencies.title: Associativity
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

nodes.title: Commutative Monoid
nodes.statement_display: A \emph{commutative monoid} is a monoid whose operation is commutative.
nodes.statement_tex: A \emph{commutative monoid} is a monoid whose operation is commutative.

direct_dependencies.title: Commutativity
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,\ x\mathbin{O}y=y\mathbin{O}x. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Monoid
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation

transitive_dependencies.title: Associativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Closure
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

nodes.title: Group
nodes.statement_display: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.
nodes.statement_tex: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

direct_dependencies.title: Monoid
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

direct_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Associativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Closure
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

ancestor_tree.title: Monoid
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

dependencies.title: Semigroup
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

dependencies.title: Left Identity
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

dependencies.title: Left Inverse
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

nodes.title: Ring
nodes.statement_display:
A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose
multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes
over addition.
nodes.statement_tex:
A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose
multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes
over addition.

direct_dependencies.title: Abelian Group
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.

direct_dependencies.title: Monoid
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

direct_dependencies.title: Ring
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. transitive_dependencies.title: Left Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

ancestor_tree.title: Ring
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

dependencies.title: Relation

A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a
multiplicative inverse.

A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Group
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

dependencies.title: Monoid
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

dependencies.title: Semigroup
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

dependencies.title: Left Inverse
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

dependencies.title: Right Inverse
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

nodes.title: Integral Domain
nodes.statement_display:
An \emph{integral domain} is a commutative ring with $0\ne1$ and no zero
divisors.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Ring
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

dependencies.title: Abelian Group
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.

dependencies.title: Commutativity
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,\ x\mathbin{O}y=y\mathbin{O}x. \]

dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

dependencies.title: Left Inverse
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

dependencies.title: Right Inverse
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

nodes.title: Ordered Field
nodes.statement_display:
An \emph{ordered field} is a field equipped with a total order compatible with
addition and multiplication by positive elements.
nodes.statement_tex:
An \emph{ordered field} is a field equipped with a total order compatible with
addition and multiplication by positive elements.

direct_dependencies.title: Ordered Field Addition Law
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ a<b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+c<b+c. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Ordered Field Multiplication Law
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ c>0 \Longrightarrow \bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow a<b\bigr), \] and \[ c<0 \Longrightarrow \bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow b<a\bigr). \] Moreover, positive elements are closed under multiplication: \[ 0<a \land 0<b \Longrightarrow 0<ab. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Ordered Field Trichotomy
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$ and $b$, exactly one of the following holds: \[ a=b,\qquad a<b,\qquad b<a. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Field
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Field Addition Law
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ a<b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+c<b+c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Field Multiplication Law
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ c>0 \Longrightarrow \bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow a<b\bigr), \] and \[ c<0 \Longrightarrow \bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow b<a\bigr). \] Moreover, positive elements are closed under multiplication: \[ 0<a \land 0<b \Longrightarrow 0<ab. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Field Trichotomy
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$ and $b$, exactly one of the following holds: \[ a=b,\qquad a<b,\qquad b<a. \]

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

ancestor_tree.title: Ordered Field Addition Law
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ a<b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+c<b+c. \]

ancestor_tree.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$ and $b$, exactly one of the following holds: \[ a=b,\qquad a<b,\qquad b<a. \]

ancestor_tree.title: Field

dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

dependencies.title: Left Identity
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

nodes.title: Positive cone
nodes.statement_display:
Let $F$ be a field. A subset $P\subset F$ is called a \emph{positive
cone} if it satisfies:
\begin{enumerate}
  \item if $a,b\in P$, then $a+b\in P$;
  \item if $a,b\in P$, then $ab\in P$;
  \item for every $a\in F$, exactly one of the following holds:
  \[
  a\in P,
  \qquad
  a=0,
  \qquad
  -a\in P.
  \]
\end{enumerate}
Define the order relation by
\[
a<b \iff b-a\in P.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
Let $F$ be a field. A subset $P\subset F$ is called a \emph{positive
cone} if it satisfies:
\begin{enumerate}
  \item if $a,b\in P$, then $a+b\in P$;
  \item if $a,b\in P$, then $ab\in P$;
  \item for every $a\in F$, exactly one of the following holds:
  \[
  a\in P,
  \qquad
  a=0,
  \qquad
  -a\in P.
  \]
\end{enumerate}
Define the order relation by
\[
a<b \iff b-a\in P.
\]

direct_dependencies.title: Field
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

direct_dependencies.title: Subset
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

direct_dependencies.title: Trichotomy
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: The relation $<$ satisfies \emph{trichotomy} if exactly one of \[ x<y,\qquad x=y,\qquad y<x \] holds for every pair $x,y$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Abelian Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Associativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Closure
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Commutative Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{commutative ring} is a ring whose multiplication is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Commutativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,\ x\mathbin{O}y=y\mathbin{O}x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Partial Order
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Strict Order
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(A,\leq)$ be an ordered set. The \emph{strict order} $<$ is defined by: \[ a < b \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; (a \leq b \land a \neq b). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Total (Connex) Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is \emph{total} (or \emph{connex}) if every pair is comparable: \[ \forall a,b \in A, \; (a,b) \in R \lor (b,a) \in R. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Trichotomy
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The relation $<$ satisfies \emph{trichotomy} if exactly one of \[ x<y,\qquad x=y,\qquad y<x \] holds for every pair $x,y$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

ancestor_tree.title: Trichotomy
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: The relation $<$ satisfies \emph{trichotomy} if exactly one of \[ x<y,\qquad x=y,\qquad y<x \] holds for every pair $x,y$.

dependencies.title: Strict Order
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(A,\leq)$ be an ordered set. The \emph{strict order} $<$ is defined by: \[ a < b \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; (a \leq b \land a \neq b). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

nodes.title: Semiring
nodes.statement_display:
A \emph{semiring} is a domain with addition and multiplication such that
addition is a commutative monoid, multiplication is a monoid, multiplication
distributes over addition, and the additive identity is absorbing for
multiplication.

distributes over addition, and the additive identity is absorbing for
multiplication.

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Binary Closure
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

nodes.title: Ordered Field Transitivity
nodes.statement_display:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$,
\[
a<b \land b<c \Longrightarrow a<c.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$,
\[
a<b \land b<c \Longrightarrow a<c.
\]

direct_dependencies.title: Ordered Field
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered field} is a field equipped with a total order compatible with addition and multiplication by positive elements.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Field Addition Law
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ a<b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+c<b+c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Field Multiplication Law
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ c>0 \Longrightarrow \bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow a<b\bigr), \] and \[ c<0 \Longrightarrow \bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow b<a\bigr). \] Moreover, positive elements are closed under multiplication: \[ 0<a \land 0<b \Longrightarrow 0<ab. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Field Trichotomy
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$ and $b$, exactly one of the following holds: \[ a=b,\qquad a<b,\qquad b<a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation

transitive_dependencies.title: Abelian Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Field
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered field} is a field equipped with a total order compatible with addition and multiplication by positive elements.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{commutative ring} is a ring whose multiplication is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

ancestor_tree.title: Ordered Field
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered field} is a field equipped with a total order compatible with addition and multiplication by positive elements.

dependencies.title: Ordered Field Addition Law
dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ a<b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+c<b+c. \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Field Multiplication Law
dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ c>0 \Longrightarrow \bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow a<b\bigr), \] and \[ c<0 \Longrightarrow \bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow b<a\bigr). \] Moreover, positive elements are closed under multiplication: \[ 0<a \land 0<b \Longrightarrow 0<ab. \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Field Trichotomy
dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$ and $b$, exactly one of the following holds: \[ a=b,\qquad a<b,\qquad b<a. \]

dependencies.title: Field
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

dependencies.title: Commutative Ring
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{commutative ring} is a ring whose multiplication is commutative.

dependencies.title: Commutativity
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,\ x\mathbin{O}y=y\mathbin{O}x. \]

dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Ring
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

dependencies.title: Abelian Group
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.

dependencies.title: Commutativity
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,\ x\mathbin{O}y=y\mathbin{O}x. \]

dependencies.title: Group
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

dependencies.title: Monoid
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

dependencies.title: Semigroup
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

dependencies.title: Left Inverse
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity

dependencies.title: Left Identity
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

dependencies.title: Right Identity
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

dependencies.title: Right Inverse
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]
